meaning: n.衬衫的胸襟,围嘴adj.恶劣的
examples: He won't say a dicky bird, but we think he knows who did it.
That ladder looks a bit dicky.
Born with a dicky heart, his protagonist was not supposed to live past the age of 30.
One commentator at the time said JFK looked like "a bronzed warrior" whereas Tricky Dicky appeared sick, unshaven and sweaty.
Dicky Fisher from Aslef said the union would continue to work with London Midland to "assist them in delivering the train service".
